notes: Bug fix via /nw-bugfix → /nw-deliver. The authoritative specification is the RCA at `docs/feature/fix-dst-dispatch-routing-invariant/deliver/bugfix-rca.md` (user-validated; explicit approval of Option A1 — mirror the dispatch in the harness via a new `drive_dispatch_routing` helper following the established `drive_broker_collapse` / `drive_broker_collapse_multi_key` pattern at `harness.rs:511-640`. Option A2 — add a `DispatchRecorder` trait to `overdrive-core` and route both production and the harness through it — was REJECTED on grounds enumerated in the RCA: dispatch routing is not a nondeterminism boundary (it is a deterministic contract), so adding a trait surface for it inflates the production hot path's argument list to satisfy a test that has a cheaper closure via Option A1).

This is NOT a defect in production code. The defect is missing test coverage at the DST tier — a structural asymmetry in the invariant catalogue (entry collapse covered via `DuplicateEvaluationsCollapse`; dispatch routing not covered) that leaves the §8 storm-proofing contract exposed under any failure mode the acceptance suite's fixed timeline cannot reach. The sibling unit/acceptance pin already exists at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs::eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler` (commit `e6f5e5e`); this work item closes the DST-tier coverage gap so the §8 contract is pinned at both unit and DST tiers per whitepaper §21.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: the new invariant variant + canonical-name arm + ALL list entry + evaluator function + supporting types + library-level witnesses + harness helper + harness dispatch arm + end-to-end test land in ONE cohesive commit. No `#[deprecated]` shadow evaluator, no two-phase rollout, no `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestrictedV1` plus `V2`. No `#[ignore]` scaffolds; no separate RED commit. The negative-case mocked fan-out scenario embedded in the end-to-end test IS the regression-test proof — it demonstrates the invariant is load-bearing without needing a separate `git commit --no-verify` step.

Single-step shape (NOT 2 steps). Unlike the precursor `fix-eval-reconciler-discarded` (which split into RED scaffold + GREEN fix because the test had to compile against the current main signature), this delivery is structurally simpler: no production-code signature change, the harness owns its own observation seam end-to-end, and the negative case in the end-to-end test demonstrates RED-equivalence in a single commit. Per CLAUDE.md "Repository structure" the new code lives entirely in `overdrive-sim` (crate_class = adapter-sim); per ADR-0004 the sim/host split is load-bearing — `overdrive-sim` MUST NOT import from `overdrive-control-plane` (which is adapter-host). The `Evaluation` mirror in the evaluators module is the established workaround pattern (parallel to `BrokerCountersSnapshot` at `evaluators.rs:679-689`).

Test placement strategy: (a) Library-level witness fns (~7 of them) live INLINE in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` next to the new evaluator function, following the existing convention for the parallel `evaluate_duplicate_evaluations_collapse` witnesses (RCA §Suggested test/invariant shape — Library-level unit witnesses; see `evaluators.rs:1142-1184` for the parallel `DuplicateEvaluationsCollapse` witness shape). (b) End-to-end harness assertion lives in `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/invariant_evaluators.rs` (the existing test file at `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/invariant_evaluators.rs`) following the existing pattern — see `entropy_determinism_passes_when_two_sim_entropies_agree` at line 199-211 of that file. NO new test file under `tests/` is needed. The roundtrip proptest at `tests/invariant_roundtrip.rs` automatically picks up the new variant via `Invariant::ALL` (per RCA §Risk assessment item 1); no test code changes required there.

Out of scope (tracked separately, NOT a step in this roadmap). Per RCA §Out of scope: (a) Multi-reconciler ticks where the broker dispatches a batch in one tick (Phase 2+); (b) Multi-region cross-Corrosion-peer dispatch routing; (c) Workflow-primitive dispatch routing (workflows have their own invariant family — replay-equivalence + bounded progress); (d) Refreshing `harness_registered_reconcilers` to match production (the literal `1` at `harness.rs:496` is now stale; `AtLeastOneReconcilerRegistered` passes under both `1` and `2` so the staleness is invisible); (e) Generalised invariants crate (ADR-0017). All five are explicitly tracked as future work; do NOT bundle them into this roadmap.

Mutation gate compatibility (note for the GREEN step). The evaluator function's three predicate branches each have a sharp mutation surface: cargo-mutants will generate (a) flipping `==` to `!=` on the cardinality check (`record.dispatched.len() == submitted.len()`) — killed by the happy-path witness (single-eval pass, multi-eval pass) AND the cardinality-fail witnesses (extra-dispatch, missing-dispatch); (b) flipping the per-eval routing match (`**d == key` vs `**d != key`) — killed by the wrong-reconciler witness AND the smoking-gun negative case in the end-to-end test; (c) replacing the smoking-gun branch's `if !submitted_names.contains(r) { return Fail }` with `return Pass` or removing it entirely — killed by the fan-out negative case in the end-to-end test (which deliberately runs an extra reconciler in the harness mirror to demonstrate the invariant fires). The `DispatchRecord` and `Evaluation` mirror types are pure data with `derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)` — minimal mutation surface (mutants typically marks data-only types as unviable).

Mutation invocation per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing — uses `run_in_background: true` (the explicit foreground exception). Command: `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-sim --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/harness.rs`. The `--features integration-tests` flag is the canonical CI flag (per testing.md §Workspace convention every workspace member declares the feature, including `overdrive-sim`); pass it explicitly. On macOS this MUST go through `cargo xtask lima run -- ...` per testing.md §Lima — although the new sim-crate code is not under `#[cfg(target_os = "linux")]`, the `--features integration-tests` flag is mandatory on every mutation invocation that uses the flag. Kill rate gate: ≥80%. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome (per testing.md §post-mutation safety).

macOS-vs-Linux invocation hygiene per `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running integration tests locally on macOS — Lima VM": (a) Default-lane invocations (`cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim`, `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-sim`, `cargo xtask dst`, `cargo xtask dst-lint`) run natively on macOS — the new code uses only sim adapters (`SimClock`, `SimEntropy`) and pure data structures, no Linux-specific kernel surface, no cgroup writes. (b) Mutation runs that pass `--features integration-tests` MUST go through `cargo xtask lima run -- ...` on macOS per testing.md §Mutation testing. The macOS-side `--no-run` typecheck (`cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run`) is a necessary but not sufficient gate.

Standing user-rule references the crafter must obey: (1) `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations` — no shims, no deprecations, no flag-gated old paths. The new invariant + helper + test land in one commit; no shadow evaluator. (2) `feedback_no_git_stash` — focused-subset commits use `git add <paths>` + `git restore --staged`, never `git stash`. (3) `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer` — clippy issues that surface during the work get fixed in-scope. (4) `feedback_no_rename_stubs` — no leftover stubs (no `panic!("Not yet implemented")` placeholders survive into the merged commit). (5) `feedback_delete_dont_gate` — no `#[cfg(test)]` annotations to silence dead-code warnings on the new types or helpers; if anything is unused after the commit lands, delete it. (6) `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running tests — foreground, always" — every test invocation goes through `Bash` with `run_in_background: false` EXCEPT mutation testing. (7) `.claude/rules/testing.md` § "Running integration tests locally on macOS — Lima VM" — `--features integration-tests` invocations on macOS go through `cargo xtask lima run -- …`. (8) CLAUDE.md "Roadmap validator warnings" — non-blocking; do NOT trim AC specificity to satisfy them. (9) CLAUDE.md "Repository structure" — `overdrive-sim` is `crate_class = adapter-sim`; the new code MUST NOT import from `overdrive-control-plane` (which is `adapter-host`). Mirror the `Evaluation` shape locally in the evaluators module. (10) ADR-0004 (sim/host split is load-bearing) — the harness `drive_dispatch_routing` helper mirrors dispatch deterministically; no real `run_convergence_tick` import.

Roadmap-validator length warnings (`STEP_NAME_TOO_LONG`, `CRITERIA_TOO_LONG`, `DESCRIPTION_TOO_LONG`) are non-blocking per CLAUDE.md "Roadmap validator warnings" — Overdrive convention deliberately carries scenario-level specificity in ACs (test names, predicate shape, file paths, branch coverage, mutation kill expectations, gate predicates). Do NOT trim ACs to satisfy these warnings.

reviews.scope: RCA + Option A1 (harness-mirror seam) vs Option A2 (core trait expansion) trade analysis. Option A1: mirror the dispatch path in the harness via a new `drive_dispatch_routing` helper following the established `drive_broker_collapse` / `drive_broker_collapse_multi_key` pattern at `harness.rs:511-640`; the harness submits a fixed set of evals naming `job-lifecycle` against distinct targets, drains, and records each dispatch as a `(reconciler, target)` tuple. Option A2: add a `DispatchRecorder` trait to `overdrive-core` and route both production and the harness through it — REJECTED on grounds enumerated in the RCA: dispatch routing is not a nondeterminism boundary (it is a deterministic contract), so adding a trait surface for it would inflate the production hot path's argument list to satisfy a test that has a cheaper closure via Option A1; preservation of the `adapter-sim` ↔ `adapter-host` boundary (no `overdrive-control-plane` import); matches the established convention three other reconciler-primitive invariants already use.
reviews.notes: User confirmed root cause (the DST invariant catalogue was sized to the single-reconciler era at step 04-05; the dispatch-routing contract was not a load-bearing property when authored because the registry was a singleton; when `JobLifecycle` joined the registry at commit `8f4aaa7` the catalogue was not extended; the gap was identified by the precursor RCA at `docs/feature/fix-eval-reconciler-discarded/deliver/bugfix-rca.md:290-292` and DELIBERATELY DEFERRED; tracked only in feature-doc references, not as a scheduled work item, until this `/nw-bugfix` flow). User APPROVED Option A1 specifically over Option A2 on the architectural grounds enumerated in the RCA — "dispatch routing is not a nondeterminism boundary; it is a deterministic contract. Adding a trait surface for it inflates the production hot path's argument list to satisfy a test that has a cheaper closure (Option A1) — the trade is poor." Single-cut migration shape confirmed: invariant variant + ALL list entry + canonical-name arm + evaluator + mirror types + library witnesses + harness helper + harness dispatch arm + end-to-end test land in one cohesive commit, no shims, no separate RED scaffold (the negative case in the end-to-end test IS the RED-equivalent proof). Out of scope explicitly: multi-reconciler ticks, multi-region dispatch, workflow-primitive dispatch routing, refreshing `harness_registered_reconcilers`, ADR-0017 generalised invariants crate — all tracked separately. Reference RCA: `docs/feature/fix-dst-dispatch-routing-invariant/deliver/bugfix-rca.md`. The user-validated RCA names the precursor commit `e6f5e5e` as the unit/acceptance-tier sibling pin; this work item closes the DST tier so the §8 storm-proofing contract is pinned end-to-end at both tiers per whitepaper §21.

phases.name: Add DST invariant `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` pinning the §8 storm-proofing dispatch-routing contract — DST-tier sibling to the unit/acceptance pin at `runtime_convergence_loop.rs::eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler` (commit `e6f5e5e`)
phases.description: Single-step Outside-In TDD bugfix landing the §8 dispatch-routing DST invariant in one cohesive commit. The new invariant `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` asserts: for any drained `Evaluation { reconciler: R, target: T }`, exactly one reconciler — R — runs through the dispatch path against T per tick (per RCA §Proposed fix — Option A1). The diff comprises FOUR file edit clusters in the `overdrive-sim` crate (which is `crate_class = adapter-sim` per CLAUDE.md "Repository structure"): (1) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/mod.rs` — add the `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` enum variant alongside the existing reconciler-primitive invariants (after `ReconcilerIsPure` per RCA §Catalogue addition), add it to `Invariant::ALL` constant in the same block (`mod.rs:114-118`), add the canonical-name arm `Self::DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted => "dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted"` in `as_canonical()` (`mod.rs:138-143`). The doc-comment on the variant cites the precursor commit `e6f5e5e` and names the invariant as the DST sibling to the unit/acceptance pin at `runtime_convergence_loop.rs::eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`. (2) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` — add the `Evaluation` mirror struct (mirrors `overdrive_control_plane::eval_broker::Evaluation` rather than importing it; sim crate stays a leaf adapter; uses `ReconcilerName` and `TargetResource` from `overdrive-core` which the sim crate already depends on), add the `DispatchRecord` snapshot struct (one entry per dispatched `(reconciler, target)` tuple), add the `evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(submitted: &[Evaluation], record: &DispatchRecord) -> InvariantResult` evaluator function with three predicate branches: (a) vacuous-pass on empty input, (b) cardinality check (`record.dispatched.len() == submitted.len()`), (c) per-eval routing check (each submitted `(R, T)` matched by exactly one entry in `record.dispatched`), (d) smoking-gun check (no dispatched entry names a reconciler outside the submitted set — catches the registry-iteration regression shape the precursor fix closed). The evaluator returns `InvariantResult { name: "dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted", status: Pass | Fail, host: CLUSTER_HOST, cause }` per the existing convention. Library-level unit witnesses (~7 of them, one per branch + edge case) live inline in the same file, each tests a single contract failure shape: `dispatch_routing_passes_on_clean_single_eval`, `dispatch_routing_passes_on_clean_multi_eval_distinct_targets`, `dispatch_routing_passes_vacuously_on_empty_input`, `dispatch_routing_fails_on_cardinality_mismatch_extra` (extra dispatch — fan-out smoking gun), `dispatch_routing_fails_on_cardinality_mismatch_missing` (deficit), `dispatch_routing_fails_on_unsubmitted_reconciler_dispatched` (smoking-gun branch), `dispatch_routing_fails_when_named_reconciler_not_dispatched` (wrong-routing). (3) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/harness.rs` — add `drive_dispatch_routing()` helper following the established `drive_broker_collapse_multi_key` shape at `:588-640` (the helper takes no arguments, constructs a fixed set of evals naming `job-lifecycle` via `ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle")` against two distinct `TargetResource` values, mirrors the dispatcher in-place by iterating the submitted evals and pushing each `(eval.reconciler.clone(), eval.target.clone())` into a `Vec<(ReconcilerName, TargetResource)>`, returns `(Vec<evaluators::Evaluation>, evaluators::DispatchRecord)`). Add the dispatch arm in `Harness::evaluate`'s match (after the `BrokerDrainOrderIsDeterministic` arm at `:419-431`): `Invariant::DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted => { let (submitted, record) = drive_dispatch_routing(); evaluators::evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(&submitted, &record) }`. (4) `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/invariant_evaluators.rs` — add an end-to-end test (or two: a happy-path test and a negative-case test) that drives the new evaluator through fixture inputs. The happy path: construct one `Evaluation { reconciler: "job-lifecycle", target: "job/payments" }`, build a `DispatchRecord { dispatched: vec![(r_jl, t_a)] }`, assert `evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(&[eval], &record).status == InvariantStatus::Pass`. The negative case (the regression-test proof): construct the same single `Evaluation`, build a `DispatchRecord { dispatched: vec![(r_jl, t_a), (r_noop, t_a)] }` (mocked fan-out — an extra reconciler runs against the same target), assert `.status == InvariantStatus::Fail` AND `.cause` contains a substring naming the smoking-gun (e.g., `"unsubmitted reconciler"` or `"fan-out"`). The negative case IS the regression-test proof — it demonstrates the invariant is load-bearing without needing a separate RED scaffold commit. NO new test file under `tests/`; NO new `mod` declaration needed (the test file already exists at `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/invariant_evaluators.rs`); existing imports cover `InvariantStatus` and `evaluators`. Single-cut migration: all four file clusters land in ONE cohesive commit. No `#[deprecated]` shadow evaluator, no two-phase rollout, no `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestrictedV1`/`V2`. NO `#[ignore]` scaffolds anywhere; no separate RED commit; the negative-case end-to-end test IS the regression-test proof. Quality gates per ACs: `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0; `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-sim` exits 0; `cargo xtask dst` exits 0 (and exercises the new invariant via at least one scenario — confirm by grepping the dst scenario list for `dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted` once the variant ships); `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0 (the new code uses only `core`-class types — `ReconcilerName`, `TargetResource` — no banned-call surface in the sim crate); `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` clean on `overdrive-sim`; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS. Mutation gate (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per testing.md §Mutation testing exception, on macOS via `cargo xtask lima run -- ...` per testing.md §Lima): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-sim --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/harness.rs` reports kill rate ≥80%; the witness fns + end-to-end negative case kill at minimum the three predicate-branch mutations enumerated in roadmap notes. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Commit message: `test(sim): add DST invariant pinning §8 dispatch-routing contract` with body citing the RCA path (`docs/feature/fix-dst-dispatch-routing-invariant/deliver/bugfix-rca.md`) and the precursor commit `e6f5e5e`; trailer `Step-ID: 01-01`. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).

steps.name: Land DST invariant `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` (catalogue + evaluator + mirror types + library witnesses + harness helper + harness dispatch arm + end-to-end happy/negative tests) in one cohesive commit
steps.description: Apply the RCA §Proposed fix — Option A1 in a single cohesive commit. The diff comprises FOUR file edit clusters; all in `overdrive-sim` (which is `crate_class = adapter-sim` per CLAUDE.md "Repository structure"). The new code MUST NOT import from `overdrive-control-plane` (which is `adapter-host`) — the `Evaluation` mirror is the established workaround pattern. CLUSTER 1 — `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/mod.rs`: (1) Add `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` enum variant after `ReconcilerIsPure` (which lives at the end of the reconciler-primitive block; current line ~72 in the existing enum). The variant carries a multi-line doc-comment matching the existing convention: "phase-1-control-plane-core / fix-eval-reconciler-discarded follow-up. For any drained `Evaluation { reconciler: R, target: T }`, exactly one reconciler — R — runs through the dispatch path against T per tick. The DST-tier peer of the unit/acceptance pin at `crates/overdrive-control-plane/tests/acceptance/runtime_convergence_loop.rs::eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`. Closes the §8 storm-proofing dispatch-routing contract end-to-end. Sibling to `DuplicateEvaluationsCollapse`: that invariant pins broker-side entry collapse, this one pins dispatcher-side routing." (2) Add `Self::DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` to `Invariant::ALL` (current `mod.rs:107-124`); placement after `Self::ReconcilerIsPure` (`:118`) keeps the reconciler-primitive block contiguous. (3) Add the canonical-name arm `Self::DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted => "dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted"` in `as_canonical()` (current `mod.rs:130-149`); placement after `Self::ReconcilerIsPure => "reconciler-is-pure"` (`:142`). The proptest at `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/invariant_roundtrip.rs` iterates `Invariant::ALL` and exercises `Display ↔ FromStr` round-trip — the new variant is picked up automatically with no test code changes (per RCA §Risk assessment item 1). CLUSTER 2 — `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs`: (1) Add the `Evaluation` mirror struct in a new `// DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` section (parallel to the existing `// DuplicateEvaluationsCollapse` section at `:679`): `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)] pub struct Evaluation { pub reconciler: ReconcilerName, pub target: TargetResource }` with a doc-comment explaining it mirrors `overdrive_control_plane::eval_broker::Evaluation` rather than importing it (sim crate stays a leaf adapter per CLAUDE.md crate classes; same discipline `BrokerCountersSnapshot` follows). The `ReconcilerName` and `TargetResource` types are sourced from `overdrive-core` (which `overdrive-sim` already depends on; verify the imports are already present at the top of `evaluators.rs`, otherwise add them). (2) Add the `DispatchRecord` snapshot struct: `#[derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)] pub struct DispatchRecord { pub dispatched: Vec<(ReconcilerName, TargetResource)> }` with a doc-comment explaining it captures one `(reconciler, target)` tuple per dispatcher invocation; sibling to `BrokerCountersSnapshot` and `BrokerDrainOrderSnapshot`. (3) Add the evaluator function `pub fn evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(submitted: &[Evaluation], record: &DispatchRecord) -> InvariantResult` with `#[must_use]`. Body has FOUR branches in this exact order per RCA §Suggested test/invariant shape: (a) Vacuous-pass: `if submitted.is_empty() { return result(name, InvariantStatus::Pass, CLUSTER_HOST, None); }` — the invariant is "for every drained eval..." and ∀∅ holds trivially. (b) Cardinality check: `if record.dispatched.len() != submitted.len() { return Fail with cause "expected {submitted.len()} dispatch entries (one per drained eval), got {record.dispatched.len()}: dispatched={record.dispatched:?}"; }`. (c) Per-eval routing check: `for eval in submitted { let key = (eval.reconciler.clone(), eval.target.clone()); let matches = record.dispatched.iter().filter(|d| **d == key).count(); if matches != 1 { return Fail with cause "expected exactly one dispatch of ({eval.reconciler}, {eval.target}) — the named reconciler — got {matches} entries: dispatched={record.dispatched:?}"; } }`. (d) Smoking-gun check: `let submitted_names: BTreeSet<&ReconcilerName> = submitted.iter().map(|e| &e.reconciler).collect(); for (r, t) in &record.dispatched { if !submitted_names.contains(r) { return Fail with cause "dispatcher invoked unsubmitted reconciler {r} against {t} — fan-out regression"; } }`. Final: `result(name, InvariantStatus::Pass, CLUSTER_HOST, None)`. The local `name` binding is `let name = "dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted";` at the top of the function. (4) Add the library-level witness fns inline in the same file, in a `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` block (or in the existing `mod tests` block if one already wraps the file's witnesses; locate by grep). Seven tests, one per branch + edge case per RCA §Library-level unit witnesses: `dispatch_routing_passes_on_clean_single_eval` (one eval `(jl, payments)`, one matching dispatch entry, expect Pass); `dispatch_routing_passes_on_clean_multi_eval_distinct_targets` (two evals against distinct targets, two matching dispatch entries, expect Pass); `dispatch_routing_passes_vacuously_on_empty_input` (empty submitted slice, empty dispatched vec, expect Pass — vacuous-pass branch); `dispatch_routing_fails_on_cardinality_mismatch_extra` (one submitted, two dispatched — extra entry; expect Fail with cause containing "expected 1 dispatch entries" or substring matching the cardinality message); `dispatch_routing_fails_on_cardinality_mismatch_missing` (two submitted, one dispatched; expect Fail same shape); `dispatch_routing_fails_on_unsubmitted_reconciler_dispatched` (one submitted `(jl, payments)`, two dispatched — `[(jl, payments), (noop, payments)]` — fails cardinality first; ALTERNATIVE: one submitted `(jl, payments)`, one dispatched `(noop, payments)` — fails the per-eval routing check because the JL eval has zero matches; the smoking-gun branch catches the case where cardinality matches but routing doesn't); `dispatch_routing_fails_when_named_reconciler_not_dispatched` (one submitted `(jl, payments)`, one dispatched `(noop, frontend)` — the JL eval has zero matches in `record.dispatched`, so the per-eval routing check fails). Each witness uses `assert_eq!(result.status, InvariantStatus::Pass)` or `assert_eq!(result.status, InvariantStatus::Fail)` and the Fail variants additionally assert on a substring of `result.cause`. CLUSTER 3 — `crates/overdrive-sim/src/harness.rs`: (1) Add `drive_dispatch_routing()` helper following the established shape of `drive_broker_collapse_multi_key` at `:588-640`. Function signature: `fn drive_dispatch_routing() -> (Vec<evaluators::Evaluation>, evaluators::DispatchRecord)`. Body: build a `Vec<evaluators::Evaluation>` containing two evals naming `ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle").expect("valid name")` against two distinct `TargetResource` values (e.g., `TargetResource::new("job/payments")` and `TargetResource::new("job/frontend")`); mirror the dispatcher by iterating the submitted vec and pushing `(eval.reconciler.clone(), eval.target.clone())` into a `Vec<(ReconcilerName, TargetResource)>`; return the pair. The helper carries a doc-comment matching the existing broker-mirror discipline: "Drive the dispatch path for the `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` invariant. Mirrors `lib.rs:465-481`'s post-fix shape (drain_pending → for eval in pending → run_convergence_tick) without depending on `overdrive-control-plane`. Each drained eval dispatches exactly one `(R, T)` where R is the eval's named reconciler. This mirrors the FIX shape (post-`e6f5e5e`); a regression in the real production dispatcher is caught by the acceptance test at `runtime_convergence_loop.rs::eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler`. The mirror's role is the SAT-side witness that the contract is satisfiable on a clean run, exactly as `drive_broker_collapse` proves the broker's collapse invariant is satisfiable." (2) Add the dispatch arm in `Harness::evaluate`'s match block (after the `BrokerDrainOrderIsDeterministic` arm at `:419-431`): ```Invariant::DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted => { let (submitted, record) = drive_dispatch_routing(); evaluators::evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(&submitted, &record) }```. CLUSTER 4 — `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/invariant_evaluators.rs`: (1) Add the happy-path end-to-end test following the existing convention (see `entropy_determinism_passes_when_two_sim_entropies_agree` at line 199-211 of the file): `#[test] fn dispatch_routing_passes_when_each_eval_dispatches_named_reconciler_only() { ... }` — construct one or more `Evaluation` mirrors, construct a `DispatchRecord` whose entries match the submitted set 1-to-1, call `evaluators::evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(&submitted, &record)`, assert `.status == InvariantStatus::Pass` and `.cause.is_none()`. (2) Add the negative-case end-to-end test (the regression-test proof): `#[test] fn dispatch_routing_fails_on_mocked_fanout_regression() { ... }` — construct one `Evaluation { reconciler: "job-lifecycle", target: "job/payments" }`, construct a `DispatchRecord { dispatched: vec![(r_jl, t_payments), (r_noop, t_payments)] }` (mocked fan-out — the harness deliberately runs an extra `noop-heartbeat` reconciler against the same target, simulating the registry-iteration regression shape the precursor fix closed at the unit tier), call the evaluator, assert `.status == InvariantStatus::Fail` AND `.cause.as_deref().unwrap_or("")`.contains("expected 1 dispatch entries") OR `.contains("unsubmitted reconciler")` (cardinality fails first because `dispatched.len() == 2` and `submitted.len() == 1`; the cardinality message names the count discrepancy). The crafter MAY split this into two negative tests if cleaner: one for cardinality (extra entry — the cardinality message), one for the smoking-gun branch (cardinality matches, smoking gun fires — e.g., one submitted `(jl, payments)`, one dispatched `(noop, payments)` — fails the per-eval routing check because the JL eval has zero matches; this is the wrong-routing shape the per-eval branch catches). ALL four clusters land in ONE cohesive commit. Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`. No `#[deprecated]` shadow evaluator, no two-phase rollout. NO `#[ignore]` scaffolds; the negative case is the regression-test proof. Commit message: `test(sim): add DST invariant pinning §8 dispatch-routing contract` with body citing the RCA path (`docs/feature/fix-dst-dispatch-routing-invariant/deliver/bugfix-rca.md`) and the precursor unit-tier sibling commit `e6f5e5e`; trailer `Step-ID: 01-01`. Normal pre-commit hooks apply (no `--no-verify`).
steps.criteria: Production sim changes (single cohesive commit) — exactly the four edit clusters enumerated in the description: (1) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/mod.rs` — `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` enum variant added with a doc-comment naming the precursor unit-tier sibling and citing commit `e6f5e5e`; variant added to `Invariant::ALL` constant; canonical-name arm `Self::DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted => "dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted"` added to `as_canonical()`. Verified by `git grep -n 'DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted' crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/mod.rs` returning ≥3 matches (variant decl, ALL list, as_canonical arm). (2) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` — `pub struct Evaluation { pub reconciler: ReconcilerName, pub target: TargetResource }` mirror added with `derive(Debug, Clone, PartialEq, Eq)`; `pub struct DispatchRecord { pub dispatched: Vec<(ReconcilerName, TargetResource)> }` snapshot struct added with the same derives; `pub fn evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(submitted: &[Evaluation], record: &DispatchRecord) -> InvariantResult` with `#[must_use]` evaluator function added with FOUR predicate branches in order: vacuous-pass on empty input, cardinality (`record.dispatched.len() == submitted.len()`), per-eval routing (`record.dispatched.iter().filter(|d| **d == key).count() == 1` for each submitted eval), smoking-gun (no dispatched entry names a reconciler outside the submitted set). Verified by `git grep -n 'evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted\|pub struct Evaluation\|pub struct DispatchRecord' crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs` showing 3+ definitions. ~7 library-level witness fns added inline in the same file (exact names per the description); each test is a single `#[test]` that calls the evaluator with a hand-crafted fixture and asserts `.status` plus a substring of `.cause` for the Fail variants. (3) `crates/overdrive-sim/src/harness.rs` — `fn drive_dispatch_routing() -> (Vec<evaluators::Evaluation>, evaluators::DispatchRecord)` helper added with a doc-comment matching the broker-mirror convention at `:574-583`; helper body constructs the submitted set (two evals naming `job-lifecycle` against two distinct `TargetResource` values) and mirrors the dispatcher's call shape (one entry per submitted eval, named by the eval). Dispatch arm `Invariant::DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted => { let (submitted, record) = drive_dispatch_routing(); evaluators::evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(&submitted, &record) }` added to `Harness::evaluate`'s match block after the `BrokerDrainOrderIsDeterministic` arm. Verified by `git grep -n 'drive_dispatch_routing\|Invariant::DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted' crates/overdrive-sim/src/harness.rs` returning ≥3 matches. (4) `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/invariant_evaluators.rs` — at least 2 `#[test]` fns added: one happy-path `dispatch_routing_passes_when_each_eval_dispatches_named_reconciler_only` (or similar) asserting `.status == InvariantStatus::Pass`; one negative-case `dispatch_routing_fails_on_mocked_fanout_regression` (or similar — the crafter MAY split this into two negative tests covering both cardinality and smoking-gun branches separately) asserting `.status == InvariantStatus::Fail` AND substring-asserting on `.cause`. Verified by `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim -E 'test(dispatch_routing)'` reporting all dispatch_routing-named tests as PASS (NOT ignored). Test verification: (1) `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` exits 0 — no compile errors anywhere. (2) `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0 with all `dispatch_routing` named tests reported as PASS (the witness fns inline in `evaluators.rs` AND the end-to-end tests in `tests/invariant_evaluators.rs`). (3) `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS. (4) `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-sim` exits 0. (5) `cargo xtask dst` exits 0; the new invariant is exercised by at least one DST scenario via `Invariant::ALL` (verified by `cargo xtask dst --json` or by inspecting `target/xtask/dst-summary.json` for the `dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted` name appearing under at least one scenario's invariant list — if the dst summary doesn't enumerate per-invariant pass/fail, fall back to inspecting the harness's `Invariant::ALL` iteration in `harness.rs:262-292`'s evaluate-all loop, confirming the new arm is reached). (6) `cargo xtask dst-lint` exits 0. The new code uses only `core`-class types (`ReconcilerName`, `TargetResource` from `overdrive-core`) plus existing `overdrive-sim` types (`InvariantResult`, `InvariantStatus`, `CLUSTER_HOST`); no `overdrive-control-plane` import; no banned-call surface (the sim crate is `adapter-sim` and is not scanned by dst-lint per CLAUDE.md "Repository structure" but the absence of `overdrive-control-plane` imports is verified by `git grep -n 'overdrive_control_plane' crates/overdrive-sim/` returning ZERO matches in the diff outside any pre-existing dev-dep usage). (7) `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` exits 0 on `overdrive-sim`. The four-branch evaluator passes clippy without any `#[allow(...)]` annotations; if a clippy lint surfaces (e.g., `#[allow(clippy::missing_errors_doc)]` or similar), it's fixed in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`, NOT silenced by suppression. (8) The roundtrip proptest at `crates/overdrive-sim/tests/invariant_roundtrip.rs` continues to pass — it iterates `Invariant::ALL` automatically and the new variant participates in the `Display ↔ FromStr` round-trip without test code changes (per RCA §Risk assessment item 1). Verified by `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim --test invariant_roundtrip` exits 0. (9) Mutation gate (post-GREEN, run with `run_in_background: true` per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing exception, on macOS via `cargo xtask lima run -- ...` per testing.md §Lima): `cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-sim --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/harness.rs` reports kill rate ≥80%. Three predicate-branch mutations expected to be killed: (a) flipping `==` to `!=` on the cardinality check killed by happy path + cardinality witnesses (extra/missing variants — the result flips from Pass to Fail or vice versa); (b) flipping the per-eval routing match (`**d == key` to `**d != key`) killed by the wrong-reconciler witness AND the smoking-gun negative case in the end-to-end test; (c) replacing the smoking-gun `if !submitted_names.contains(r)` branch with a no-op (or `return result(...Pass...)`) killed by the fan-out negative case in the end-to-end test which deliberately runs an extra reconciler in the harness mirror. Run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. No shim, no shadow evaluator, no `#[ignore]` scaffolds. acceptance_criteria: RCA §Defect (closure pin) — the §8 storm-proofing dispatch-routing contract is now pinned at the DST tier via the new `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` invariant. The whitepaper §21 primary safety net (deterministic simulation under permuted schedules / partition windows / turmoil multi-host topology) now exercises the same contract as the precursor's unit/acceptance pin (commit `e6f5e5e`). End-to-end coverage: broker-side entry collapse pinned by `DuplicateEvaluationsCollapse` + dispatcher-side routing pinned by `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestricted` jointly cover the §8 contract; neither subsumes the other; both are required.

RCA §Root cause closure — the DST invariant catalogue is no longer sized to the single-reconciler era. The new variant joins the four existing reconciler-primitive invariants (`AtLeastOneReconcilerRegistered`, `DuplicateEvaluationsCollapse`, `BrokerDrainOrderIsDeterministic`, `ReconcilerIsPure`) at the same enum block in `invariants/mod.rs`, in `Invariant::ALL`, and in `as_canonical()`. The catalogue's structural asymmetry (entry collapse covered, dispatch routing not) is closed.

RCA §Proposed fix — Option A1 — the implementation matches the RCA's specified code shape exactly: `evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted(submitted: &[Evaluation], record: &DispatchRecord) -> InvariantResult` with FOUR branches in order — vacuous-pass on empty input, cardinality check (`record.dispatched.len() == submitted.len()`), per-eval routing check (each submitted `(R, T)` matched by exactly one `record.dispatched` entry), smoking-gun check (no dispatched entry names a reconciler outside the submitted set). The harness `drive_dispatch_routing` helper mirrors the production dispatcher in-place — no `overdrive-control-plane` import, preserving the `adapter-sim` ↔ `adapter-host` boundary per ADR-0004.

RCA §Risk assessment items 1-7 closure — (1) the roundtrip proptest at `tests/invariant_roundtrip.rs` continues to pass with no test code changes (the new variant is picked up automatically via `Invariant::ALL`); (2) the dep graph is preserved (no `overdrive-sim` → `overdrive-control-plane` import); (3) the smallest-possible scope expansion is achieved — two pub structs in the evaluators module, one new evaluator function, one new enum variant, one new match arm, one new harness helper, ~100 lines total; (4) the new invariant runs default-on via `Invariant::ALL`; (5) mirror-vs-production drift is mitigated by the existing acceptance test (`runtime_convergence_loop.rs::eval_dispatch_runs_only_the_named_reconciler` runs against the real dispatcher); (6) mutation testing eligible — the evaluator function is pure with sharp predicate branches; (7) Phase 2+ generalisation is straightforward (cardinality and smoking-gun checks are already permutation-invariant on the target axis).

RCA §Suggested test/invariant shape — the 7 library-level witness fns are inline in `evaluators.rs` covering: clean single-eval pass, clean multi-eval-distinct-targets pass, vacuous-pass-on-empty pass, cardinality-extra fail, cardinality-missing fail, smoking-gun-unsubmitted-reconciler fail, wrong-routing fail. The end-to-end harness assertion in `tests/invariant_evaluators.rs` covers happy path AND mocked fan-out negative case; the negative case IS the regression-test proof, demonstrating the invariant is load-bearing without needing a separate `#[ignore]`-attributed RED scaffold commit.

Single-cut migration per `feedback_single_cut_greenfield_migrations`: enum variant + ALL entry + canonical-name arm + evaluator function + mirror types + library witnesses + harness helper + harness dispatch arm + end-to-end happy-path test + end-to-end negative-case test all land in ONE cohesive commit. No `#[deprecated]` shadow evaluator, no `DispatchRoutingIsNameRestrictedV1`/`V2`, no two-phase rollout, no `panic!("Not yet implemented")` placeholders. The negative-case end-to-end test IS the RED-equivalent regression-test proof — no separate RED commit, no `git commit --no-verify`.

All existing tests across the workspace remain GREEN: `cargo nextest run --workspace` exits 0 (the witness fns AND the end-to-end tests pass; the existing reconciler-primitive invariants pass unchanged); `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-sim` exits 0; `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` typechecks on macOS; `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim --features integration-tests` exits 0 on Linux directly (or on macOS via `cargo xtask lima run -- ...`). The roundtrip proptest at `tests/invariant_roundtrip.rs` continues to pass with no test code changes.

steps.implementation_notes: TDD phase mapping within this step: PREPARE EXECUTED — re-read RCA §Proposed fix — Option A1, §Code shape, §Suggested test/invariant shape, §Files affected. Confirm current line numbers in `crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/mod.rs` (the RCA cites `:53-66` for the existing reconciler-primitive doc-comments, `:107-124` for `Invariant::ALL`, `:130-149` for `as_canonical`); locate insertion points by symbol name and grep, not by absolute line number — the file may have drifted slightly. Confirm the existing imports at the top of `evaluators.rs` already include `ReconcilerName` and `TargetResource` from `overdrive_core`; if not, add them. Confirm the existing imports at the top of `harness.rs` cover `ReconcilerName` and `TargetResource` (or add them — the broker-mirror helpers `drive_broker_collapse*` already use these, so they should be available). Confirm the existing imports at the top of `tests/invariant_evaluators.rs` already include `overdrive_sim::InvariantStatus` and `overdrive_sim::invariants::evaluators` (verified by `head -30` of the file in the agent's earlier context — both imports are present at lines 24 and 27). RED_ACCEPTANCE EXECUTED in same commit — the negative-case end-to-end test in `tests/invariant_evaluators.rs` constructs a mocked fan-out scenario and asserts `.status == InvariantStatus::Fail`; this is the structural RED-equivalent proof, demonstrating the invariant is load-bearing without needing a separate `#[ignore]`-attributed RED commit. RED_UNIT EXECUTED in same commit — the witness fns inline in `evaluators.rs` cover the four predicate branches with discrete fail cases (cardinality-extra, cardinality-missing, wrong-routing, smoking-gun); each is a `#[test]` that calls the evaluator with a hand-crafted Fail-shaped fixture and asserts `.status == InvariantStatus::Fail` plus a substring of `.cause`. GREEN EXECUTED in same commit — implement in this order: (1) Add the `Evaluation` mirror struct and `DispatchRecord` snapshot struct in `evaluators.rs` (parallel to the existing `BrokerCountersSnapshot` at `:679`). (2) Add the `evaluate_dispatch_routing_is_name_restricted` function with the FOUR branches per RCA §Code shape: vacuous-pass, cardinality, per-eval routing, smoking-gun. The local `name = "dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted"` binding goes at the top of the function. The `result(...)` helper used elsewhere in the file is the canonical Pass/Fail constructor — reuse it. (3) Add the witness fns inline in the same file in the existing `#[cfg(test)] mod tests { ... }` block (or a new one if the file doesn't yet have one for this section — locate by grep). Each witness builds a `submitted` slice and a `record` and calls the evaluator; expected results match the test name. (4) Edit `invariants/mod.rs`: add the enum variant after `ReconcilerIsPure`; add to `Invariant::ALL` after `Self::ReconcilerIsPure`; add the canonical-name arm after `Self::ReconcilerIsPure => "reconciler-is-pure"`. The doc-comment on the variant follows the existing convention — multiline, cites the precursor commit `e6f5e5e`, names the unit-tier sibling test, and explains the §8 storm-proofing dispatch-routing contract. (5) Edit `harness.rs`: add the `drive_dispatch_routing` helper following the `drive_broker_collapse_multi_key` shape at `:588-640`. The helper constructs two evals with `ReconcilerName::new("job-lifecycle").expect(...)` against two `TargetResource::new("job/payments")` and `TargetResource::new("job/frontend")` (use `expect` per the existing convention in the broker-mirror helpers). The mirror's dispatcher loop is `for eval in &submitted { dispatched.push((eval.reconciler.clone(), eval.target.clone())); }` — one entry per submitted eval, named by the eval. Add the dispatch arm in `Harness::evaluate`'s match block (after `BrokerDrainOrderIsDeterministic`). (6) Edit `tests/invariant_evaluators.rs`: add the happy-path `#[test] fn dispatch_routing_passes_when_each_eval_dispatches_named_reconciler_only()` and the negative-case `#[test] fn dispatch_routing_fails_on_mocked_fanout_regression()` (the crafter MAY split the negative case into two tests if cleaner — one for cardinality-extra, one for smoking-gun). Each test imports the `Evaluation` and `DispatchRecord` types from `overdrive_sim::invariants::evaluators` (the public module path). (7) Run `cargo check --workspace --all-targets` — should compile clean. (8) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace` — all tests including the new `dispatch_routing`-named ones should pass. (9) Run `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-sim` — green. (10) Run `cargo nextest run -p overdrive-sim --test invariant_roundtrip` — the new variant participates in the roundtrip via `Invariant::ALL` automatically; should pass without test code changes. (11) Run `cargo xtask dst` — green; the new invariant runs via the harness's evaluate-all loop. Inspect `target/xtask/dst-summary.json` for `dispatch-routing-is-name-restricted` appearing in at least one scenario's invariant list. (12) Run `cargo xtask dst-lint` — green (no `overdrive-control-plane` import in `crates/overdrive-sim/`; verified by `git grep -n 'overdrive_control_plane' crates/overdrive-sim/`). (13) Run `cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings` — green; fix any clippy issues in-scope per `feedback_fix_clippy_dont_defer`. (14) Run `cargo nextest run --workspace --features integration-tests --no-run` (macOS typecheck) — green. COMMIT EXECUTED — `git commit` (no `--no-verify`); commit body cites the RCA path, the precursor unit-tier sibling commit `e6f5e5e`, and explicitly enumerates the four edit clusters. Single cohesive commit. POST-GREEN MUTATION RUN: per `.claude/rules/testing.md` §Mutation testing exception, mutation runs use `run_in_background: true`. On macOS, the invocation MUST go through `cargo xtask lima run --` per testing.md §Lima because `--features integration-tests` is in the command. Invoke `cargo xtask lima run -- cargo xtask mutants --diff origin/main --features integration-tests --package overdrive-sim --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/invariants/evaluators.rs --file crates/overdrive-sim/src/harness.rs`. Wait for completion via the shell ID; parse `target/xtask/mutants-summary.json` for the kill rate. Expected mutations to be killed (3 distinct classes per roadmap notes): (a) cardinality `==` flipped to `!=` killed by happy-path + cardinality witnesses (extra and missing variants); (b) per-eval routing match `**d == key` flipped to `**d != key` killed by the wrong-reconciler witness + smoking-gun negative case; (c) smoking-gun branch's `if !submitted_names.contains(r) { return Fail }` replaced with `return Pass` (or removed) killed by the fan-out negative case in the end-to-end test which deliberately constructs a `DispatchRecord` containing an unsubmitted reconciler. On any surviving mutation, add a concrete test case that disambiguates it AND re-run mutation — NOT weaken existing assertions, NOT relax the predicate logic. Always run `git checkout -- crates/` after the mutation pass terminates regardless of outcome. Record phase transitions via `python3 -m des.cli.log_phase`. Post-commit smoke check: re-run `cargo nextest run --workspace`, `cargo test --doc -p overdrive-sim`, `cargo xtask dst`, and `cargo xtask dst-lint` one more time to confirm zero regressions; this is the input to Phase 3.5 post-merge integration gate. CRITICAL crafter callouts: (1) The `Evaluation` mirror lives in `evaluators.rs`, NOT in `mod.rs` — keep all evaluator-specific types co-located with their evaluator. (2) The harness `drive_dispatch_routing` helper is a free function (not a method on `Harness`); follow the existing `drive_broker_collapse` / `drive_broker_collapse_multi_key` shape at `:511-640` exactly. (3) The crafter MAY add a small `#[test]` block inside `harness.rs` (parallel to `drive_broker_collapse_multi_key_returns_expected_snapshot` at `:785`) verifying that `drive_dispatch_routing` returns the expected pair shape — but this is OPTIONAL; the end-to-end test in `tests/invariant_evaluators.rs` and the witnesses inline in `evaluators.rs` already pin the contract. (4) Do NOT update `harness_registered_reconcilers` to refresh the stale `1` literal — that is explicitly out of scope per RCA §Out of scope item (d). (5) Do NOT introduce a new test file under `tests/` — the existing `tests/invariant_evaluators.rs` is the correct landing point per RCA §Files affected — Test code. (6) The `result(...)` helper invoked by every existing evaluator is already in scope in `evaluators.rs` — reuse it, do NOT inline construct `InvariantResult { ... }` literals. (7) Mutation runs on macOS go through `cargo xtask lima run --` per testing.md §Lima — the `--features integration-tests` flag is the trigger for the Lima requirement, not the actual code-under-test being Linux-specific. (8) Single-cut: NO `#[ignore]` attribute anywhere; NO `#[deprecated]` shadow evaluator; NO `panic!("Not yet implemented -- RED scaffold")` placeholders survive into the merged commit; the negative-case end-to-end test IS the RED-equivalent regression-test proof.
